title: Initial position: path optimization filter
description: Starting position with all three dice rolled: Pawn (1), Knight (2), and Bishop (3). According to the Dice Chess maximum micro-moves rules, quiet a/c/f/h pawn moves are completely filtered out because they do not form or enable a valid 3-move sequence (e.g. Pawn -> Bishop -> Knight).

title: Pawns with three dice
description: In this position, the white pawn on d5 can move to c6, d6, or e6 using one of the '1' dice. The white pawn on e7 can promote to a knight, bishop, queen, or rook on d8, e8, or f8 using the remaining two '1' dice.

title: Casting and bishop moves with three dice
description: In this position, the black king on e8 can castle kingside to g8, or move to f8. The black bishop on e7 can move to f8, d6, c5, b4, or a3. The black rook on a8 can move to b8

title: Force promotion to knight or bishop
description: Forcing a pawn to promote to a knight or bishop. Maximum two micro moves in this position.

title: Force promotion to knight or rook
description: Forcing a pawn to promote to a knight or rook. Maximum two micro moves in this position.

title: Force promotion to knight or queen
description: Forcing a pawn to promote to a knight or queen. Maximum two micro moves in this position.

title: Force promotion to knight only
description: Forcing a pawn to promote to a knight only.

title: Beat the king or promote to a knight
description: We can beat the king or promote to a knight. Two or three micro moves in this position.

title: Bishop and king moves with three dice
description: In this position, the black king on e8 can move to f8 or d8. The black bishop on d7 can move to c6, b5, a4, e6, f5, g4, or h3.
